rulings.Case Title: STANLEY PUCHOSIC VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 16, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corp.¿s motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 1 (premises owner/contractor liability) and denied as to issue 2 (punitive damages). As to issue 1, as pled, plaintiff¿s cause of action for premises owner/contractor liability, as it relates to contractors, does not require a showing that defendant owned, operated, maintained or controlled the premises. While this likely means that there is no substantive difference between plaintiffs¿ causes of action for negligence and premises owner/contractor liability, that is not a basis to grant summary adjudication and is more appropriately handled by the trial judge through jury instructions or otherwise. As to issue 2, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant punitive damages. Plaintiff¿s responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:30am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: MAXIMILIANO RAZO et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Consolidate Actions For All Purposes Including Trial
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 16, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2, Plaintiffs¿ motion for order to consolidate Action No. CGC-09-275293 with Action No. CGC-11-275937 is granted. Consolidation is proper under CCP § 1048(a) as it will enhance the trial court¿s economy and efficiency while avoiding unnecessary delay. However, the consolidation will not affect the rights of the parties, particularly the defendants¿ rights to conduct meaningful discovery. Henceforth, all papers shall be filed in the lead case number: Action No. CGC-09-275293. The December 20, 2012 Trial Setting Conference date set is maintained. Discovery remains closed for the parties in Action No. CGC-09-275293 unless there¿s a showing of good cause. The February 6, 2012 trial date given to Action No. CGC-09-275293 is vacated. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:30am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
